description_long: This is a small, rectangular cabin with a simple, compact structure and a gable roof. The roof is dark brown and appears to be made of shingles or tiles, contrasting with the light-colored walls, which are likely constructed from wood or a similar material. The front facade features two small, square windows symmetrically placed on either side of a central door, all with dark trim. The side walls are plain, with no visible windows or doors, emphasizing the minimalistic design. The cabin sits on a flat, circular base, suggesting it is intended as a standalone structure. Its overall appearance is modest and functional, suitable for residential or recreational use in an urban or suburban setting.
description: A small, rectangular cabin with a dark gable roof, light walls, and a symmetrical front featuring two windows and a central door. The design is simple and functional, with minimal exterior detailing.
description_view_0: Front view showing two square windows flanking a central door, all with dark trim on a light wall.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain light wall with no windows or doors, and the dark gable roof above.
description_view_2: Top-down view highlighting the rectangular footprint and the dark, shingled gable roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with a plain light wall and the gable roof visible.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch, covered in dark shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: Two small, square windows symmetrically placed on the front facade, one on each side of the central door; no windows visible on the sides or rear
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door centered on the front facade between the two windows
